A.does: PikeVM works by simulating an NFA and resolving all spans of capturing groups that participate in a match (pikevm.rs:4-5).

B.does: BoundedBacktracker works by simulating an NFA using the classical backtracking algorithm with a twist: it avoids redoing work that it has done before and thereby avoids worst case exponential time (backtrack.rs:5-7).

comparison: BoundedBacktracker can be faster than PikeVM in many cases because it does less book-keeping, but in exchange it can only be used on 'short' haystacks (backtrack.rs:8-10).
